questions.question: What is the time complexity of the Brute Force algorithm for string matching?
options: O(n log n) for all inputs
questions.explanation: The Brute Force algorithm checks every possible substring of the target string, resulting in a time complexity of O(n) for all inputs.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of Sequential Search?
options: Can be used for general pattern matching
questions.explanation: Sequential Search is a linear algorithm that only checks for exact matches and does not support general pattern matching.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'bad character hypothesis' in the Brute Force algorithm?
options: To increase the number of possible matches
questions.explanation: The 'bad character hypothesis' is used to eliminate characters that cannot match, reducing the search space and improving the algorithm's efficiency.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of the Brute Force algorithm?
options: It is highly efficient for small inputs
It is highly inefficient for large inputs
questions.explanation: The Brute Force algorithm is highly inefficient for large inputs due to its exponential time complexity, making it less suitable for large-scale string matching tasks.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of the Sequential Search algorithm?
options: It is suitable for general pattern matching
questions.explanation: Sequential Search is highly accurate and simple to implement, making it a good choice for exact string matching tasks where precision is crucial.
